task: explain how auth and identity checks work right now for the trails command

Currently, the trail command in @cmd/entire/cli/trail_cmd.go  and types in @cmd/entire/cli/trail/trail.go rely on trails being stored in git branches and makes corresponding git operations for read write operations on trails. With https://github.com/entirehq/entire.io/pull/1115 we are deprecating trails stored in git and instead moving it all to the API. create a new branch based of the API auth integration in  https://github.com/entireio/cli/pull/692 which uses the new APIs for manipulating trails, keeping the existing trail command syntax and supported operations. if APIs are missing in the backend to achieve certain operations, highlight them.
Similar to the move from git to the db in the backend, this should be non-destructive, i.e. on the first operation using trails in a repo, migrate trails to the use of the API instead of git, place a marker file in the orphan entire/trails/v1 branch
user_feedback_text:
Currently, the trail command in @cmd/entire/cli/trail_cmd.go  and types in @cmd/entire/cli/trail/trail.go rely on trails being stored in git branches and makes corresponding git operations for read write operations on trails. With https://github.com/entirehq/entire.io/pull/1115 we are deprecating trails stored in git and instead moving it all to the API. create a new branch based of the API auth integration in  https://github.com/entireio/cli/pull/692 which uses the new APIs for manipulating trails, keeping the existing trail command syntax and supported operations. if APIs are missing in the backend to achieve certain operations, highlight them.
Similar to the move from git to the db in the backend, this should be non-destructive, i.e. on the first operation using trails in a repo, migrate trails to the use of the API instead of git, place a marker file in the orphan entire/trails/v1 branch
